item.title: Fjord horse
item.description:
The Fjord or Norwegian Fjord Horse (Norwegian: fjordhest) is a relatively small but very strong horse breed from the mountainous regions of western Norway. It is an agile breed of light draught horse build. It is always dun in colour, with five variations in shade recognised in the breed standard. One of the world's oldest breeds, it has been used for hundreds of years as a farm horse in Norway, and in modern times is popular for its generally good temperament. It is used both as a harness horse and under saddle. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fjord_horse
#Fjord_horse #horse #norway #frozen #animals #nature
item.dc:creator: TongueTiedLabourerfromtheLandoftheLittlePeople

item.title: Time for the unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, friends of the online variety
I’ve been attacked
attacked yet again
sad really
no need to brag
I’m still not convinced
one of life’s great mysteries
I want to see the wizard
my whole existence is flawed
mmm hmm
hope that person is ok
perfect time for a cig
check out the Mormon nerds in Utah
we’ve all been there
well we already have rum ham at least
obviously
finally a book for this
that’s all for me, have a great weekend gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Please help in Denver area
item.description: My niece is missing and I live half a country away. Thanks everyone. I appreciate you all. Also connected to the Parker area. Use these numbers, don't report sightings in the comments. Thank you. CBI missing persons (303) 239-4649 DPD non-emergengy line (720) 913-2000 Edit: she finally reached out to my sister in law and is safe and sound.
item.dc:creator: HeyILikeDogs

item.title: Eyebleach for these trying times
item.description: Recently I had the privilege to work on a research mobile app to help with suicide prevention. Part of this includes a chat and we had to build in profanity filtering. This meant loading up a list of known swears, inuendos, and racial slurs...and my God. I needed to scrub my eyes after reading such lists! So here's a dump of eyebleach: cute animals, relaxing scenes, and altogether warm feelings to send you on your way! Part of the study works to confirm the statistics of social media interaction and the positive impact it can have on suicide prevention. The theory being: the more connected you are to peers, the greater sense of a belonging, and the lower the risk of self harm. That's what makes places like Imgur potentially great! We just need that social connection, and keep positive posts like this flowing in! And if you're finding yourself reflecting on thoughts of self harm, call 988 (in the US). They're trained professionals willing to talk to you. If you're looking for a quick chat from someone who will never judge, DM me! I'm always happy to learn about folks, your backgrounds, interests, and just how beautiful we can be as human beings.

item.dc:creator: JebidiahBeetus

item.title: Welcome to Funny Friday! A humorous dump to end the workweek
item.description:
It's time for Funny Friday! As always when I do these dumps, there is a link in the comments to a private album with some bonus material, which I do just to make sure these posts don't get flagged mature for petty reasons, and out of respect for anyone browsing at work. Also in the comments is a quick thing if you want to be pinged when I make these posts, because I don't put them out every week. So if you want a notification when I do, please let me know.
... Aurora Borealis?
Peach has to be careful with Mario. He's delicate.
Now we know why they sounded so scared about the snake.
Truly an illustration of the duality of birds. (Peace really never is an option for the goose)
Any bets? I think the one thing that Spock and McCoy can agree on is how irritating Kirk can be.
Ironic that this was posted by The Shovel, since that tool won't do any good!
F1nn doesn't need a pet, he is the pet.
You have to be careful while crowdsurfing, the tide can pull you under.
I love the detail with the changes. Especially how all you need to do is rearrange letters to turn moe into emo.
George R.R. Martin said words are wind. I say you should break some.
You have to account for the momentum that hasn't been lost, Peter.
Yep, that's classic Caesar all right.
The Balrog is Imhotep, Pippin is Rachel Weisz's annoying brother, Aragorn is the Arab guy who was guarding the tomb, what else am I missing?
Probably giving away how much of an old man I am by including this.
That's a lot of... EMOTIONAL DAMAGE!
To be honest, I'd probably crack bad jokes like these at least a few times if I was original enough to come up with them.
It was in that moment that he knew... he fucked up.
The dog conditioned Pavlov well.
Some puns are so bad but I love them anyway.
Just in case the prior image didn't convince you I was telling the truth. (Also, I'd totally buy and wear this shirt)
Judging by the engineers I've known, this would be a good thing.
... yeah.
The lengths they have to go to get weebs to shower.
"Too old!" - Leo, probably
That's... that's not wrong...
Every time.
Every GODDAMN time! Can I get a little backup here, guys?
If they have a problem with the gay agenda, just wait until we get to the trans mission!
I wonder what her evil laugh will sound like.
He's a cunning linguist and had to shoot his shot. It was a good attempt.
I don't have a cat, so this lovely boy the the stars on his fur is the closest you get to a cat tax. Have a great weekend!
item.dc:creator: politicalwanderer

item.title: Ever notice how they've never once actually solved a problem?
item.description: After New Gingrich’s Republican Revolution in 1994 the Republicans have held the House for 22 of the past 30 years. They held the Senate 16 of the 30 years. And when Trump was sworn in, he had a GQP house and Senate. Even W had a Republican Senate and House. Yet they haven’t put together their own border laws when given the chance, and won’t let Biden enact a law.
item.dc:creator: ButtBot9900

item.title: First goal of the new year achieved!
item.description: It's not by a large margin, but I've been weighing myself all week (unhealthy habit, I know) and I've been consistently under 200lbs for a few days now and I'm super proud of myself. As someone who has been overweight (and considered obese as a child and teen) I'm over the moon. I have about another 30lbs to go until I think I'll be satisfied with my weight loss goal, but I think I'm in the right groove with my eating habits, that I should achieve my goal by the end of the year, if not sooner- which is super exciting because I've really been looking forward to getting a tummy tuck as part of my transition. I can't wait to buy my first pair of pants that aren't in the double digits. Anyways, I just figured I'd share my good news with all of you. And in case no one has told you today or if you don't hear it enough, I love you and am proud of you. As always: shine brightly, beautiful people.
item.dc:creator: MatronOfMorgues

item.title: MEGAFAUNA that modern humans encountered
item.description:
For the purposes of this post, modern humans means our ancestors, from the successful migrations out of Africa 60,000 - 90,000 years ago, to today. It encompasses the ethnic diversity of our common African heritage, Cro-Magnons, Australian Aborigines, the Clovis People of America, the first Asians, and others. They are us, and they encountered amazing creatures that no longer exist on Earth. ————————————— In zoology, the term megafauna can refer to any creature over 46kg, or alternatively over 1 tonne. This post uses both measures with the exception of Flores Man.
GLYPTODON - Giant Armadillo Length: 4 metres - 13 feet long Weight: 2.4 tonnes - 2.6 tons ————————————— Co-existed with early South American people for 2,000 - 3,000 years, and lived alongside Smilodon and other sizeable predators for 3.2 million years.
Went extinct about 11,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human predation.
MEGALANIA - Giant Monitor Lizard Length: 7m - 23 feet long Weight: 1.9 tonnes - 2 tons ————————————— The largest terrestrial lizard known to have existed. Co-existed with Aboriginal Australians for up to 25,000 years. Carnivorous with a poisonous bite.
Went extinct in Australia about 40,000 years ago during the Australian Megafauna Extinction, possibly from a combination of climate change and human competition.
WOOLLY MAMMOTH Height: 3.5 meters - 11.5 feet at the shoulder Weight: 8.2 tonnes - 9 tons Tusks 4.5 metres - 15 feet long ————————————— Co-existed with early Europeans and their descendants for about 15,000 years in Siberia, Mongolia, China, Europe. And with the Clovis People in North America for several thousand years.
Most species went extinct about 11,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human predation. ————————————— One population of pygmy mammoths survived on Wrangel Island in the Arctic Ocean until 4,000 years ago. They probably went extinct due to a fresh water drought caused by local environmental change.
WOOLLY RHINOCEROS Length: 3.6 metres - 12 feet long Weight: 2 tonnes - 2.2 tons Horn 1.35 metres - 4.4 feet long ————————————— Co-existed with early Europeans and their descendants for about 15,000 years in Siberia, Mongolia, China, Europe. And with the Clovis People in North America for several thousand years. Cave paintings show them being hunted with spears and arrows.
Went extinct about 14,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly due to a combination of climate change and human predation.
MEGALOCEROS - Irish Elk Height: 2.1 metres - 7 feet at the shoulder Weight: 700kg - 1540lb Antlers 3.7 meters - 12 feet tip-to-tip ————————————— Co-existed with early Europeans across Europe and Asia, and were depicted in the 17,300 year old Lascaux Cave paintings.
Most Megaloceros went extinct about 12,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human predation. ————————————— One small enclave survived in Siberia till 7,000 - 8,000 years ago.
NEANDERTHALS Height: Up to 1.75m - 5’7” tall Weight: Up to 82kg - 180lbs ————————————— They had larger brains than modern humans, although the reasons for their brain size may not correlate directly to intelligence, but also include increased management requirements for their body type and survival habits.
They spread widely across Eurasia and were successful big game hunters using spears. Neanderthal skeletons often show signs of injuries consistent with regular contact with large creatures. A measure of their evolutionary success is that unlike many other species, they resisted Homo Sapiens’ territorial expansion for a long time. When early archaic modern humans first left Africa they did not immediately spread out across the rest of the globe as might be expected. Instead, they were restricted to, and co-existed with Neanderthals in parts of Europe and Asia for 100,000 years. Today 5% of all non-African human DNA is Neanderthal.
Neanderthals went extinct about 40,000 years ago - the last known survivors in a remote outpost in modern Gibraltar. Their extinction is likely due to multiple factors - human competition, climate change, natural catastrophes, interbreeding and hybridisation.
FLORES MAN Height: 106cm - 3’7” tall Weight: 32.5kg - 72lbs ————————————— Likely co-existed with the early Asian people in modern-day Indonesia for a brief period of time. ————————————— Flores man had a small brain roughly the size of a chimpanzee’s, but used stone tools and may have used fire. They were also known to have hunted pygmy elephants.
Went extinct 50,000 years ago, around the time of arrival of first modern humans. Their disappearance coincides with the extinction of other large fauna in the region, and has been hypothesised to have occurred due to the arrival of those modern humans.
CAVE HYENA Height: 1 meter - 39” at the shoulder Weight: 103kg - 230lbs ————————————— Ranged from South Western Europe to Siberia and preyed on megafauna such as horses, bison, and woolly rhino. Neanderthals and Hyenas competed for cave habitation, and early humans hunted and were preyed upon by Cave Hyenas. ————————————— Artist credits: Roman Yevseyev and Julio Lacerda
Went extinct between 10,000 - 30,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human competition.
CAVE BEAR Height: 4 meters - 13 feet tall on their hind legs Weight: 1000kg - 2,200lbs ————————————— Territory included Europe and Asia, where they were sometimes hunted by or fought with humans and neanderthals. They were mostly herbivorous, as well as opportunistic omnivores. Their bones have been found in prehistoric human cave dwellings along with many other animals species.
Went extinct about 24,000 years ago, after a long period of population decline over the previous 25,000 years, coinciding with the first arrival of modern humans in their territories. Although they were hunted, the frequency of this very dangerous activity was unlikely to have caused their extinction. Instead, the increase of human populations may have led to habitat competition.
SMILODON POPULATOR - Largest Smilodon species Height: 1.2 metres - 4 feet at the shoulder Weight: A single skull was discovered in Uruguay in 1989, far larger than any previous sabertooth, and analysis by palaeontologists calculated it belonged to an individual that weighed 436kg - 960lb. They possessed fangs up to 30cm long. ————————————— Ranged over large areas of South America, hunting megafauna such as bison, camels, deer, and tapir. There is no evidence of Smilodon consistently hunting humans, or vice versa. Smilodon species co-existed alongside ancestral humans for 300,000 years or more, but humans were the apex predator for much of that time - as evidenced by their ability to kill and then spend time butchering large prey uninterrupted by other predators. ————————————— Artist credit: Gabriel Ugueto
Went extinct about 10,000 years ago during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human predation.
CAVE LION Height: 1.2 meters - 4 feet at the shoulder Length: 2.5 meters - 8’2” long not including the tail Weight: 340kg - 750lbs ————————————— Co-existed with early Europeans in Western Europe, northern Eurasia, and Canada. They were depicted in the Chauvet cave paintings 17,000 years ago. Humans and neanderthals hunted the cave lion, preserving the pelts, claws, and bones as trophies. The American Lion was another species with similar size and characteristics that inhabited large parts of the North American continent. ————————————— It was called the Cave Lion not because it lived in caves, but because one of its chief prey were cave bears, and many lion skeletons have been found in cave bear habitats. ————————————— Artist credit: Velizar Simeonovski
Went extinct about 13,000 years ago, probably in the Alaska Yukon area, during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human competition.
THYLACALEO CARNIFEX - Marsupial Lion Height: 75cm - 30” at the shoulder Weight: 164kg - 362lbs ————————————— About the size of a modern lioness, it co-existed with Aboriginal Australians for about 25,000 years. ————————————— Artist credit: Roman Yevseyev
Went extinct about 40,000 years ago, during the Australian Pleistocene megafauna extinction, with the cause or causes being speculated as climate or habitat change and competition from humans. Uniquely amongst predator extinctions, no other non-human mammalian apex predator rose to replace them.
HOMOTHERIUM - Scimitar Tooth Cat Height: 1.1 meters - 3’7” at the shoulder Weight: 190kg - 420lb ————————————— About the size of a modern male lion, it co-existed with early Europeans and Clovis People in a large territory across North America, South America, Eurasia, and Africa. ————————————— Faster than Smilodon and probably a pursuit hunter, it adapted to higher altitudes and colder temperatures than most other cat species. At Friesenhahn Cave in Texas, the remains of almost 400 juvenile mammoths were discovered with numerous Homotherium skeletons of all ages, indicating they were a common prey for the cats. As pack hunters, it is likely they hunted both humans and neanderthals, and were hunted in turn.
Went extinct in North America about 12,000 years during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and human competition.
MEGATHERIUM - Giant Ground Sloth Height: 2.1 meters - 6’11” at the shoulder Weight: 4.6 tonnes - 5 tons Length: 6 meters - 20 feet head to tail ————————————— Co-existed with the Clovis People in its territory across South America. Was about the size of a modern Asian Elephant.
Went extinct about 12,000 years during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of habitat loss, climate change, and human predation.
ARGENTAVIS - Giant Teratorn Height: 1.8 meters - 5’11” when standing Wingspan: 8 meters - 26 feet Weight: 80kg - 176lb It was the heaviest known flying bird of all time ————————————— Ranged across South America, co-existing with Clovis People for 4,000 - 5,000 years.
Went extinct about 10,000 years during the global megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, possibly from a combination of climate change and competition from smaller, faster birds.
DROMORNIS Height: Over 3 meters - 9’8” in height Weight: 584kg - 1,287lb ————————————— Larger than the New Zealand Moa and the Madagascar Elephant Bird, Dromornis inhabited the north of the Australian continent and co-existed with Aborigines for up to 35,000 years.
Went extinct about 30,000 years ago, possibly due to a combination of climate change, human intervention, and competition with smaller flightless bird species.
HAAST’S EAGLE & THE MOA Haast’s Eagle wingspan: 2.6 - 3 meters - 8’6” - 9’10” Moa height: 3.6 meters - 12 feet tall Moa weight: 230kg - 510lb ————————————— Both species co-existed with Polynesians in New Zealand for about 100 years.
Haast's Eagles primarily ate Moa, other birds, and small mammals, possibly including human children.
Haast's eagle went extinct about 1400AD, driven to extinction in little more than a century by human competition for prey.
The Moa went extinct about 1445AD, hunted to extinction within 100 years of the arrival of the Polynesian people.
QUINKANA - Land Crocodile Length: One specimen found was 6 meters - 20 feet in length Weight: 400kg - 880lb ————————————— Coexisted with Aboriginal Australians for about 55,000 years. Quinkana had long legs and is thought to have been a fast-moving persistence hunter.
Went extinct about 10,000 years ago, with climate change the most likely factor, their semi-aquatic territory changing to grassland after the last glacial cycle.
STELLER’S SEA COW Length: 9 meters - 30 feet Weight: 9 tonnes - 10 tons Was the largest known member of the manatee and dugong family. ————————————— Co-existed with Siberian Yupik and other Inuit people for 10,000 years. Wide-ranging territory during the Pleistocene, but found only an isolated region of the Bering Sea when discovered by Russian sailors in 1741.
Went extinct from hunting within 27 years of discovery, although unconfirmed sightings persisted until the 1970’s.
PROCOPTODON Height: 2.7 meters - 8’10” tall Weight: 240kg - 530lbs ————————————— Co-existed with Aboriginal Australians for 20,000 - 47,000 years ————————————— Too large to hop due to tendon stresses, so locomotion may have been bipedal like hominids ————————————— Artist credit: Michael Long
Went extinct in Australia 18,000 - 45,000 years ago, possibly due to climate change and hunting by humans. However there is no existing fossil evidence of predation or consumption by humans.
ELASMOTHERIUM Height: 2.5 meters - 8’2” at the shoulder Length: 4.5 meters - 15’, not including the horn Weight: 5 tonnes - 5.5 tons ————————————— Co-existed with humans and neanderthal across its territory in Eastern Europe and Central Asia.
Went extinct 35,000 - 50,000 years ago in the Kazakhstan region, around the same time as the Neanderthals - possibly due to climate change and low population numbers.
DIPROTODON Height: 1.8 meters - 5’11” at the shoulder Length: 4 meters - 13 feet long Weight: 3.5 tonnes - 3.85 tons ————————————— Co-existed with Aboriginal Australians for about 25,000 years. Was the largest marsupial known to have existed.
Went extinct in Australia about 40,000 years ago during the Australian megafauna extinction, possibly due to climate change and human predation. However there is currently no fossil evidence of predation or consumption by humans.
SHORT FACED BEAR Height: 3.4 meters - 11 feet tall, on hind feet Weight: 950kg - 2,090lb ————————————— Co-existed with the Clovis People across its North American territory for several thousand years. Processed bones from Short Faced Bears have been found at Clovis sites.
Went extinct about 12,800 years ago during the megafauna extinction at the end of the Pleistocene, likely due to human competition, climactic factors, and low genetic diversity.
item.dc:creator: ivymantled69

item.title: Doordarsh steals my package
item.description:
Unbelivable! What a dumb ass!
Both Petco and Doordash have been informed. Doordash has the video... what a dumb ass. He'll lose his job over $200 worth of clothes. Hope it was worth it.

item.title: Adam Kinzinger: Donald Trump is literally insane.
item.description: Adam Kinzinger served as a United States representative from Illinois from 2011 to 2023. A member of the Republican Party.
